text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village, there was a shopkeeper named Olga who loved nothing more than telling silly stories she invents. Her favorite story involves a cat wearing a tiny astronaut helmet who explorers the stars in a tiny spacecraft.

On one magical afternoon, as Olga stood behind her counter strewn with ancient tomes and peculiar knick-knacks, a curious creature sauntered in—a fluffy, fur-covered owl with a monocle perched upon its nose. The owl had heard of Olga's fabulous tales and was eager to hear one herself.

So Olga began, her voice floating melodically as she recounted the story of Mittens, the adventuresome owl determined to become the first nocturnal space explorer.

Mittens lived in a moonlit nest high atop a ancient oak tree. Every full moon, he'd launch into orbit on what he called his ""Silent flight""—a solo expedition into the mysteries of twinkling stars.
